summary: DeVault argues forcefully against working for megacorporations like FAANG companies, contending that they don't care about individual employees and offer little meaningful autonomy or impact. He breaks down the power imbalance: employees represent a negligible fraction of revenue while facing NDAs, non-competes, and a legal system tilted toward wealth. Beyond personal risk, he argues employees become complicit in corporate harms including privacy violations, union busting, and exploitation of foreign labor. He concludes that tech workers have abundant alternatives and would likely be happier at smaller companies.
key_points: Individual employees are financially insignificant to megacorps, representing fractions of a percent of revenue, creating a fundamental power imbalance
Billion-dollar valuations aren't built through good employee treatment but through ruthlessness, monopoly exploitation, and regulatory capture
Employees face severe legal disadvantages through NDAs and non-competes, while companies can outspend them on lawyers by orders of magnitude
Working at a megacorp makes you complicit in their harmful practices, from privacy dragnets to labor exploitation
Tech workers are in high demand and have thousands of alternative employers where they'd likely be happier and more impactful
money_quotes: Why should you give a company that isn't invested in you 40+ hours of your week, half your waking life, the only life you get?

A business doesn't get a billion-dollar valuation by having a productive team which takes good care of its employees, rewarding them with interesting projects, or quickly correcting toxic work environments.
The megacorps got their 10th figure with another strategy: ruthlessness.
Megacorps are a faceless machine which is interested only in making as much money as possible with any resources at their disposal, among those being a budget which exceeds most national GDPs.
That .00075% of their annual revenue you took home? They could easily spend 100x that on lawyers without breaking a sweat, and money is justice in the United States.
They may hurt you, but even worse, they will make you hurt others.
Would you give a corporation with this much blood on its hands $750K of your worth?
The "dream job" megacorps have sold you on is just good marketing.
key_insight: Megacorps exploit their employees' idealism through good marketing while offering negligible autonomy and making workers complicit in corporate harm, when tech workers have abundant better alternatives.
